title: Granting highly privileged GCP resource rights is security-sensitive
description: Granting highly privileged roles (e.g., owner, editor) to users or service accounts in GCP increases the risk of accidental or malicious actions that can compromise the entire environment. Least privilege should always be enforced to minimize risk.
message: Highly privileged role granted. Restrict permissions to least privilege required.
impact: Highly privileged accounts can make destructive changes, access sensitive data, or escalate privileges, leading to data loss or breaches.
recommendation: Grant only the minimum permissions required for each user or service account. Avoid using owner/editor roles except when absolutely necessary.

noncompliant.description: User is granted the 'owner' role, which provides full administrative access.

compliant.description: User is granted only the 'viewer' role, following least privilege.

why_is_this_an_issue: Highly privileged roles increase the risk of accidental or malicious actions that can compromise security and compliance.
how_to_fix: Audit all IAM policies and roles. Remove unnecessary privileges and enforce least privilege. Monitor for changes to IAM configurations.
